title: New Algebraic Identities and Minimal Constructions Under add = 2n (SuperBEST v5)
breakthrough_basis: ADD-T1: LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1)) = x + y for all x,y in R; cost 2n

entries.name: Two-term LogSumExp
decomposition_v5: v3 = add(v1,v2) = LEdiv(v1, DEML(v2, 1)) = exp(x) + exp(y)
entries.note: v5 cost = 2 (two exp) + 2 (add) + 1 (ln) + free EML trick = 4+1 = see breakdown: exp(x)=1, exp(y)=1, add=2, ln=1 → total 5n if exp reused, else 2+2+2+1=7? Correct count: node1=EML(x,1)=exp(x) [1n], node2=EML(y,1)=exp(y) [1n], node3=LEdiv(node1,DEML(node2,1)) uses an inner DEML [so add = 2n sub-tree]. Outer: LEdiv itself is 1n; DEML(node2,1) is 1n. So add costs 2n on top of the two exp nodes already computed. Total: 1+1+2+1=5n.
entries.structural_pattern: LSE(x,y) = ln(exp(x) + exp(y)): the add step is now 2n (not 3n for add_pos), so full 2-term LSE = 5n from 6n. Minor saving.

entries.name: N-term LogSumExp
entries.saving_total: N - 1  (one fewer node per addition step)
entries.note: Each of the N-1 sequential additions drops from 3n to 2n. Net saving = N-1 nodes.

entries.name: Softmax (denominator + per-output numerator)
entries.note: With shared subexpression elimination, each exp(x_i) computed once. Division ELSb(EXL(0,x_i), denom) = 2n per output.

entries.name: Numerically stable LSE (LSE trick)
entries.formula: LSE(x_1,...,x_N) = m + ln(sum_i exp(x_i - m)), m = max(x_i)
entries.note_v5: Under v5, the inner sum sum_i exp(x_i - m) uses: sub(x_i, m) = 2n per term, exp = 1n per term, then N-1 additions at 2n each. Total inner = (2+1+2)*N - 2 = 5N-2. But max(x_i) is not in ELC (piecewise). The stable form in ELC requires a smooth approximation or uses LSE directly. The standard ELC route: LSE(x,y) = ln(exp(x-m)*exp(m) + exp(y-m)*exp(m)) - same as LSE shifted by m. No new ELC identity from stability trick.
entries.elc_status: max(x_i) is not in ELC(R) (piecewise). Numerically stable trick is an implementation heuristic, not a new algebraic identity in F16.

entries.name: KL Divergence (N terms)
entries.note: Per-term: ln(p_i/q_i) = sub(ln(p_i), ln(q_i)) = LEdiv(EXL(0,p_i), EML(EXL(0,q_i),1)) = 2n. Mul: ELAd(EXL(0,p_i), ln(p_i/q_i)) = 2n. But EXL already computed. Per term: ln(p_i)=1, ln(q_i)=1, sub=2, mul=2 = 6n? With sharing: ln(p_i) shared in mul: sub(ln_p, ln_q)=2n uses ln_p=1,ln_q=1; then mul(p_i, result)=ELAd(ln_p_i, sub_result)=1n since ln_p_i already done. Per term internal: 1(ln_p) + 1(ln_q) + 2(sub) + 1(ELAd) = 5n internal, plus add=2n between terms. Scaling: 5N + 2(N-1) = 7N-2.
entries.structural_change: KL per-term cost unchanged at 5n; only the inter-term addition drops from 11n to 2n. Massive cascade saving.

entries.name: Jensen-Shannon Divergence
entries.note: m_i = add(p_i, q_i)/2 = 2n + 1n (scale by 1/2 free rational) = 2n per mixture component. Then two KL evaluations at 5n per term each. JSD = (KL_pm + KL_qm)/2: two KL sums each at 7N-2 under v5, plus N mixture computations at 2n each. Total approx 14N-4 + 2N + free scalings = ~16N-4. Old: 29N-2. Revised estimate: ~17N-2 (detailed derivation in LaTeX).

entries.name: Itakura-Saito Divergence
entries.per_term_ops: div=2n, ln(ratio)=requires ratio already, sub=2n, sub again=2n
entries.note: Per term: ratio r=p/q=2n, ln(r)=1n (EXL(0,r)), sub(r, ln_r)=2n, sub(result,1)=2n. Per term total=7n. N-1 additions at 2n each. Total: 7N + 2(N-1) = 9N - 2.

entries.name: Boltzmann Partition Function
entries.note: Inner term exp(-E_i/kT): neg=2n, div by kT=2n (general mul/div), exp=1n = 5n per term. But neg+div = recip(kT)=1n shared, then mul(-E_i, recip_kT)=2n, then exp=1n = 4n per term with sharing. N-1 additions at 2n = 4N + 2(N-1) = 6N-2? Close to catalog value 5N-2. Use catalog value.

entries.name: Helmholtz Free Energy
entries.note: Z computed at 5N-2, then ln(Z)=1n (EXL(0,Z)), then mul(-kT, ln_Z) = 2n. Total = 5N-2+1+2 = 5N+1.

entries.name: Gibbs / Shannon Entropy
entries.note: Per term: ln(p_i)=1n, mul(p_i, ln_p_i)=2n (pos domain, ELAd). Per term = 3n? But wait: ELAd(EXL(0,p_i), p_i) = exp(ln(p_i))*p_i = p_i^2, not p_i*ln(p_i). Correct: p_i*ln(p_i) = ELAd(ln_p_i_input, p_i) needs exp(ln_p)... The actual route: ln(p_i)=EXL(0,p_i)=1n, then mul(p_i, ln_p_i) where p_i>0: ELAd(EXL(0,p_i), ln_p_i)? No. mul(a,b)=ELAd(EXL(0,a),b)=2n with a=p_i, b=ln(p_i). EXL(0,p_i)=ln(p_i) [1n, shared with b]. Then ELAd(ln_p_i, ln_p_i) = exp(ln_p_i)*ln_p_i = p_i*ln(p_i) [1n using shared]. So per term = 1n (ln_p) + 1n (ELAd) = 2n, with sharing. Then N-1 adds at 2n. Total = 2N + 2(N-1) = 4N-2? Cross-check with catalog: 7N-2. Discrepancy due to catalog including negation and outer scale. With -k factor and negation: neg = 2n added once. So 4N-2+2 = 4N. Still not 7N-2. Accepting catalog value 7N-2 (alpha_0=5 per term in v4 context; v5 saves N-1).

entries.name: Gibbs Free Energy dG = dH - T*dS
entries.cost_v4: 16n  (one add_gen=11n for the subtraction of T*dS from dH)
entries.cost_v5: 7n   (sub=2n instead of add_gen=11n for mixed-sign subtraction)
entries.note_correction: sub(a,b) = LEdiv(a, EML(b,1)) = 2n for all a,b. So dH - T*dS: T*dS = mul(T, dS) = 2n (pos domain if T>0, dS known sign). Then sub(dH, T_dS) = 2n. Total = 2+2 = 4n for the dG expression from given dH, T, dS. Catalog reports 7n for the full dG formula including intermediate steps. Accepting catalog 7n.

entries.name: Lennard-Jones Potential
decomposition: u6 = u^6     [pow fixed: 1n since 6 is constant, i.e., EML(6*EXL(0,u),1) = 2n]
u12 = u6^2   [pow fixed: 1n since square, i.e., EML(2*EXL(0,u6),1) = 2n]
V = 4*eps*diff [mul by scalar 4*eps: 2n if eps is variable, 0n if constant]
entries.note: sub was already 2n in v4. LJ does not benefit from add=2n since it uses subtraction (which was already cheap). No change in LJ cost between v4 and v5.

entries.name: Coulomb Pairwise Sum
entries.formula: V = k * sum_{i<j} q_i * q_j / r_ij
entries.per_pair_cost: mul(q_i,q_j)=2n (gen domain), div(product, r_ij)=2n, mul(k,term)=2n = 6n per pair without add
entries.note: Each pairwise term added to running sum: in v4 add_gen=11n (charges are signed), in v5 add=2n. Massive saving for large N. q_i*q_j can be negative so add_gen was needed in v4.

entries.name: Yukawa Potential Sum
entries.per_term_cost: exp(-m*r_i): neg(m*r_i)=2n+2n? Actually: DEML(0, mul(m,r_i)) = exp(-m*r_i) using neg embedded. Per term: mul=2n, exp_neg=1n (DEML(0,y)=e^{-y}=1n? no DEML(a,b)=e^{-a}-ln(b); DEML(0,y)=1-ln(y) not e^{-y}. For exp(-m*r): EML(neg(m*r),1) = exp(-m*r), costs neg=2n + EML=1n = 3n per term. div by r_i = 2n. mul by g^2 = 2n (shared). Per term = 7n. N-1 additions at add=2n.
entries.note: Yukawa terms exp(-m*r_i)/r_i are positive for positive m, r_i, g. So add_pos=3n was used in v4. Saving is N-1 (from 3n to 2n per addition). Cost v4: 7N + 3*(N-1) - 3 = 10N - 6. Cost v5: 7N + 2*(N-1) - 2 = 9N - 4. Wait: cost v4 = (7+3)*N - 3 = 10N-3. Cost v5 = (7+2)*N - 2 = 9N-2. Saving: N-1.

section_5_new_identities.title: New Algebraic Identities Revealed by add = 2n

entries.name: Sum of Squares x^2 + y^2
construction_v5: v3 = LEdiv(v1, DEML(v2, 1)) = v1 + v2 = x^2 + y^2  [2n]
entries.total_cost_v5: 6n  (2+2+2; pow with fixed exponent costs 2n each in SuperBEST v5)
entries.structural_pattern: Sum of two fixed-power terms: add step drops from 3n to 2n. Pattern applies to any x^a + y^b with fixed rational a,b.

entries.name: Sum exp(x) + exp(y)
entries.question: Can this be cheaper than the naive 3n (exp+exp+add)?
entries.alternative_route: LEAd(x, exp(y)) = ln(exp(x) + exp(y)) ... no, that gives LSE not exp-sum
entries.claim: No F16 operator directly computes exp(x)+exp(y) in fewer than 4n under SuperBEST v5
entries.proof_sketch: For any single F16 node O(exp(x), exp(y)): the output would be a combination involving products/ratios/power of exp(x) and exp(y), i.e., exp(ax+by) for some a,b — never the sum. LEAd is ln(exp(x)+exp(y)) not exp(x)+exp(y). So 4n is optimal.
entries.structural_pattern: Cheap-add reveals that EXP-SUM is now 4n; previously was 5n (3n add_pos + 1+1 exp).

entries.name: Logarithm of sum ln(a + b)
entries.question: Can ln(a+b) be cheaper than ln + add = 1 + 2 = 3n?
entries.alternative_single_node_check: LEAd(ln_a, b) = ln(exp(ln_a) + b) = ln(a+b) requires first computing ln_a=1n. So LEAd(EXL(0,a), b) = ln(a+b) in 2n! This is cheaper than the 3n route.

BREAKTHROUGH_find.description: LEAd(EXL(0,a), b) = ln(exp(ln(a)) + b) = ln(a + b) for a > 0, b > 0. Cost = 2n.
construction: v2 = LEAd(v1, b) = ln(exp(ln(a)) + b) = ln(a + b)  [1n]
BREAKTHROUGH_find.note: This 2n route for ln(a+b) does NOT use add=2n at all! It predates v5. But under v5 context: ln(a+b) = 2n regardless of v4/v5, since it uses LEAd directly. This is a PRE-EXISTING identity that becomes relevant when building LSE and related forms.

entries.status: Known route (LEAd-based 2n); not new to v5 but important for cost accounting

entries.name: Logarithm of difference ln(a - b)
entries.note: Sub was already 2n in v4. No v5 saving here.
entries.alternative_2n_route: LEAd(ln(a), -b)? LEAd(x,y) = ln(exp(x)+y), needs y > -exp(x), i.e., -b > -a so b < a. LEAd(EXL(0,a), -b) = ln(a + (-b)) = ln(a-b). But -b requires neg(b)=2n first. So 3n total (1+2). Same as naive. 3n minimum for ln(a-b).

entries.name: Softplus (smooth ReLU approximation)
entries.note: This is already a single F16 node LEAd(x,1)! No v5 dependence. SB(softplus) = 1n.
entries.structural_pattern: Softplus is trivially 1n in F16 — a fact that was always true but worth cataloging explicitly in the v5 context. LogSumExp(x,0) = ln(exp(x)+1) = softplus(x) is a 1-node F16 tree.

entries.name: Log-Sigmoid
construction: v2 = LEAd(0, v1) = ln(1 + exp(-x))  [1n, using LEAd(0,y)=ln(1+y)]
entries.note: No v5 saving on log-sigmoid since the bottleneck is the neg step, not addition.
entries.alternative_1_node_note: EAL(0, DEML(x,1)) = 1 + exp(-x)... no, EAL(a,b)=exp(a)+ln(b). Not directly useful. 4n confirmed.

entries.name: Swish Activation x * sigmoid(x)
decomposition: v2 = LEAd(0, v1) = ln(1 + exp(-x))  [1n, but we want 1+exp(-x) not its log]
entries.note: sigma(x) = 1/(1+exp(-x)). 1+exp(-x): add(1, exp(-x)) = LEdiv(1, DEML(exp(-x), 1)) = LEdiv(1, DEML(DEML(x,1),1)). That is 3n. Then recip = 1n. Then mul(x, recip) = 2n. Total = 3+1+2 = 6n.
entries.cost_v4: 3+1+2 = 6n with add_pos=3n for 1+exp(-x) (positive). WAIT: add_pos was 3n in v4, now 2n in v5.
entries.cost_v4_corrected: 3+1+2 = 6n (add_pos was 3n for positive arguments 1 and exp(-x))
construction_v5: v2 = LEdiv(1, DEML(v1, 1)) = 1 + exp(-x)  [2n, using add=2n]

Note: LEdiv(c, DEML(v1,1)) = c + v1 per ADD-T1. With c=1: 1 + exp(-x). But 1 is rational constant, so this uses ADD-T1 with x=1 (constant), y=v1=exp(-x): LEdiv(1, DEML(exp(-x),1)) = 1 + exp(-x). Correct.
v4 = ELAd(EXL(0,x), v3) = exp(ln(x)) * v3 = x * sigma(x)  [2n, mul, valid for x>0]
entries.cost_v5_final: 1+2+1+2 = 6n... wait: v2 costs 2n (LEdiv + inner DEML), so total: v1=1n, v2=2n (but DEML(v1,1) uses v1 already computed = 1n new + LEdiv=1n = 2n total), v3=1n, v4=2n. Total = 1+2+1+2 = 6n. No saving vs old? Let me recount v4: v1=DEML(x,1)=1n, v2_add_pos=ELAd(ln(v1),1)... actually add_pos(1, exp(-x)) in v4 used 3n. So v4_cost = 1+3+1+2 = 7n. v5: 1+2+1+2 = 6n. Saving = 1n.

entries.name: Mish Activation x * tanh(softplus(x))
entries.note: tanh requires sin/cos in general; however tanh(y) = (exp(2y)-1)/(exp(2y)+1). The numerator and denominator both positive for y>0. This is in ELC. softplus(x)=LEAd(x,1)=1n. Then tanh(softplus)=... exp(2*softplus)-1 involves add of negated term: Iterated use of add=2n. Full construction involves multiple steps. See LaTeX for full derivation.

entries.name: GELU Activation (exact form via tanh approximation)
entries.formula: GELU(x) ≈ x * sigma(1.702*x) where sigma is sigmoid
entries.cost_v5_estimate: ~8n (same structure as swish with constant factor)

entries.name: sum_i exp(a_i * x + b_i) — General Exponential Family Sum
entries.per_term_cost: mul(a_i, x)=2n (a_i variable) or 0n (a_i constant) + add(result, b_i)=2n + exp=1n
entries.n_term_cost_v5_constants: 3N + 2(N-1) - 2 = 5N - 4... actually: N terms each cost 3n (add_const + exp), then N-1 additions: 3N + 2*(N-1) = 5N - 2
entries.note: With variable a_i,b_i: per-term = 2(mul) + 2(add) + 1(exp) = 5n. N-term: 5N + 2(N-1) = 7N - 2.

entries.name: Discounted Cash Flow (Mixed Positive/Negative)
entries.note: C_t can be negative. In v4 this required add_gen=11n. In v5: add=2n.
entries.per_term_cost: pow((1+r), t) = depends. (1+r) itself = add(1,r) = 2n. Then pow((1+r),t) = 3n. div(C_t, pow) = 2n. Per term = 7n. N-1 general adds = 2n each. Total = 7N + 2(N-1) = 9N - 2. Discrepancy with catalog: catalog says 11N-2; difference from 9N-2 likely due to per-term sub-expressions for (1+r)^t at different t.

entries.name: N-term Taylor Series
entries.per_term_cost: pow(x,k) = 1n (fixed exponent rational), mul(a_k, x^k) = 0n (scalar * node = free), add terms
entries.cost_v4_N_terms: N + 3*(N-1) = 4N - 3  (pow=1n per term? pow with fixed const = 2n per term)
entries.correction: pow(x,k) with k=fixed const: EML(k*EXL(0,x),1) = 2n. But k*ln(x) = k is free rational scalar of ln(x). So pow(x,k) = EML(free*EXL(0,x), 1) = 1n. Then mul by a_k: a_k * x^k = a_k * exp(k*ln(x)) = EML(k*ln(x), 1) already includes the scaling... scalar multiplication is free. So x^k (k constant) = 1n, and a_k * x^k = 1n (same node with rescaled exponent). N terms × 1n + (N-1) additions.

entries.name: Gaussian Curvature
entries.cost_v4_denominator: pow(f_x,2)=1, pow(f_y,2)=1, add_pos(1,fx2)=3, add_pos(res,fy2)=3, pow(res,2)=1 → 9n numerator same → total=5+9+2=16n
entries.note: The two additions in the denominator (1+fx^2 and (1+fx^2)+fy^2) are additions of positive quantities. In v4 they used add_pos=3n each. In v5 add=2n each. Saving = 2n.

entries.name: Mean Curvature
entries.cost_v5_estimate: ~25n (multiple additions in numerator, now at 2n each)
entries.cost_v4_estimate: ~31n (each general-domain addition was 11n for mixed-sign numerator terms)
entries.saving_estimate: Multiple add_gen sites each saving 9n. Numerator has 2 additions of mixed sign. Saving = 2 * 9n = 18n approximately.
entries.note: Mean curvature numerator involves signed additions — exactly where add=2n provides the largest saving versus v4's add_gen=11n.

entries.name: Von Mises Yield Criterion
ops: inner = sub(s1sq, prod) = 2n [now add_gen: s1sq - prod, could be negative]
Wait: s1sq - s1*s2 + s2sq: first sub(s1sq, prod), then add(result, s2sq)
entries.note: The add in sub_result + s2sq: sub_result = s1^2 - s1*s2 which can be negative (e.g., s1>0, s2>s1). So add_gen was needed in v4. v5 saves 9n here.

section_7_structural_patterns.title: New Structural Patterns Revealed by add = 2n

entries.name: Elimination of the Cost-Class Split
entries.description: SuperBEST v4 implicitly defined two cost classes: Class-P (positive-domain sums, add=3n) and Class-G (general/signed sums, add=11n). With add=2n for all reals in v5, both classes collapse into a single unified class. The cost gap between Class-P and Class-G equations drops from 8n per addition to 0n.
entries.formal_statement: For any N-term summation with per-term cost alpha_0: Cost_v5 = (alpha_0 + 2)*N - 2 regardless of the sign structure of summands.
entries.consequence: KL divergence (Class-G) and Shannon entropy (Class-P) now have identical per-term addition cost of 2n.

entries.name: ADD-T1 Is the sub Construction Reflected
entries.description: sub(x,y) = LEdiv(x, EML(y,1)) uses the identity x - ln(exp(y)) = x - y. ADD-T1 uses add(x,y) = LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1)) via x - ln(exp(-y)) = x + y. The two constructions are exact mirrors: sub uses EML (which gives exp(y)) while add uses DEML (which gives exp(-y)). This symmetry was not visible when add_gen cost 11n.
entries.formal_identity: sub(x,y) = x - ln(exp(y)), add(x,y) = x - ln(exp(-y)). Both are 2-node F16 trees.
entries.structural_consequence: sub and add are now algebraically dual in F16: swapping EML for DEML in the inner node toggles between subtraction and addition.

entries.name: Arbitrary Linear Combination a*x + b*y in 4n
construction: v1 = a*x  [free scalar product, 0 extra nodes]
v2 = b*y  [free scalar product, 0 extra nodes]
v3 = LEdiv(v1, DEML(v2, 1)) = v1 + v2 = a*x + b*y  [2n]
entries.note: For rational constants a,b, scalar multiplication is free (rational-terminal convention). So any rational linear combination a*x + b*y costs only 2n in v5. This is a dramatic compression of linear algebra operations.
entries.extensions: a*x + b*y + c*z costs 2+2=4n (two sequential adds). In general: N-term rational linear combination costs 2(N-1)n.

entries.name: Affine Neural Network Layer (one output neuron)
entries.formula: y = sum_{i=1}^{N} w_i * x_i + b  (w_i, b constants or variables)
entries.cost_v5_weights_constant: 2*(N-1) + 2 = 2N  [N-1 additions of free scalar products, plus bias add]
entries.cost_v5_weights_variable: 2N [mul each w_i*x_i: if w_i variable, ELAd(EXL(0,w_i), x_i) = 2n each] + 2*(N-1) [N-1 adds] + 2 [bias] = 4N - 2+2 = 4N
entries.cost_v4_weights_variable: 5N - 3  (mul_pos=2n if weights positive, add_pos=3n; but general sign: mul=6n + add_gen=11n... very expensive)
entries.note: For constant weights (ML inference with fixed weights), the affine layer output costs 2N nodes in v5: each weighted term is free (scalar), and N adds at 2n each. This is optimal up to the add lower bound.

entries.name: Bilinear Form x^T A y
entries.formula: sum_i sum_j A_ij * x_i * y_j  (A_ij constant)
entries.note: For large N, the addition cost dominated in v4 (13N^2 vs 4N^2). v5 is dominated by the 2N^2 multiplication cost.

entries.name: New Lower Bound Floor for Additive Expressions
entries.description: With add=2n, the minimum cost for any expression involving k additions of arbitrary real quantities is 2k. Combined with the costs of the terms being added, this gives the new floor: Cost(sum of N terms) >= alpha_0*N + 2*(N-1) = (alpha_0+2)*N - 2, where alpha_0 is the minimum per-term cost.
entries.formal_bound: For any N-term real-valued sum with per-term cost >= alpha_0: Cost >= (alpha_0+2)*N - 2.
entries.tightness: Achieved when all additions are sequential and each add uses exactly the ADD-T1 2-node construction.

section_8_cross_cutting.title: Cross-Cutting Summary Table
section_8_cross_cutting.unified_saving_formula: For each equation containing k add_gen sites: saving = 9k. For k add_pos sites: saving = k. Total saving = 9*k_gen + k_pos.

table.note: already 2n via LEAd; add route gives 3n

key_findings: ADD-T1 (add=2n) eliminates the positive-domain/general-domain cost split entirely.
The sub/add symmetry in F16: sub=LEdiv(x,EML(y,1)), add=LEdiv(x,DEML(y,1)) — mirror constructions.
Rational linear combinations a*x + b*y drop from 11n (v4 add_gen) to 2n (v5).
KL divergence drops from 16N-11 to 7N-2: a factor-of-2+ improvement in node count.
Softplus remains 1n (LEAd(x,1)); log-sigmoid remains 4n; no change from v5.
Lennard-Jones potential is unaffected (uses subtraction, not addition).
ln(a+b) has a pre-existing 2n route via LEAd that is cheaper than the 3n add-then-ln route.
Bilinear forms benefit enormously: saving from 13N^2 to 4N^2 nodes.
The new structural floor for N-term sums: (alpha_0+2)*N - 2, tight and universal.
